title: node-redis createClient with socket.rejectUnauthorized: false — the Redis TLS certificate is not validated (MITM)

params.passDetail: The node-redis client validates the server certificate (socket.rejectUnauthorized is not false) — the rediss:// connection's TLS identity is verified.
params.failDetail: createClient is configured with socket: { rejectUnauthorized: false }, disabling validation of the Redis server's TLS certificate. A network attacker can present a forged certificate and man-in-the-middle the connection — reading and tampering with cached sessions, rate-limit counters, and queues. Remove rejectUnauthorized: false; trust the provider's CA via socket.ca.
params.absentCallDetail: No node-redis createClient call in this scope; this rule does not apply here.
params.absentArgDetail: createClient does not set socket.rejectUnauthorized; the secure default (validate) applies.

provenance.note: The node-redis (v4) twin of the ioredis footgun. socket: { rejectUnauthorized: false } is pasted to connect to a managed Redis whose CA isn't configured locally, and ships — disabling MITM protection on the store that often holds session state.
